kba_problem_summary: Windows 'Signing out' screen can remain active for 15+ minutes during logoff on DeltaV workstations (local, remote, thin client or Terminal Server sessions) due to hanging processes or modal dialogs
severity_justification: Minor usability degradation limited to the logoff phase; no impact on process control, production, data integrity or safety systems.
occurrence_justification: Requires specific conditions (certain applications running, ShellExperienceHost.exe behavior, remote/thin client usage) that are plausible but not guaranteed in all sessions.
detectability_justification: The persistent 'Signing out' screen makes the issue immediately visible to the operator attempting to log off.

modifiers_applied.reason: Multiple documented workarounds (Ctrl+Alt+End and PowerShell logoff script via Group Policy)

affected_products: SE2519 Dell Wyse Z90DE7 Thin Client (various models)
workaround_description: Press Ctrl+Alt+End to access options and close hanging apps, or deploy PowerShell script to kill ShellExperienceHost.exe on logoff via Local/Domain Group Policy
divergence_explanation: Emerson classifies this as 'General Product Technical Information' with 'Required Action: As Needed', which aligns with an Informational risk level
notes: Issue has been tracked since 2018 and remains applicable through DeltaV v15.FP3. No safety-related components or field/control layer (levels 0-1) are involved.
